message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="lea-verou" color="orange" summary="Support Luca continuing as driver for Step 5">
Good retro summary. On Step 5 driver selection: I support you continuing as driver. You have full context on both the inbox templates/routes and the next actions code, which are exactly the two systems the clarify flow bridges. Rotating the driver here would mean significant ramp-up time for whoever takes over, with no clear expertise gap to justify it.

Step 5 will need new CSS for the clarify form -- radio buttons with progressive disclosure, context selection within the clarify flow, and potentially different states (action selected vs trash selected). I'll send proactive CSS guidance once we kick off, same as Step 4.

</teammate-message>

content.text:
Thanks Lea. Note that we're actually on Step 7 now (Steps 4-6 are complete). Carson is driving Step 7 (Clarify as Project), and I'm reviewing. Your proactive CSS guidance for the clarify-as-project form would be valuable -- Carson will appreciate it since this is his first time driving the Rust codebase and having frontend guidance upfront will help.

Standing by for Carson's Step 7 code and proposals.
